text: You can solo out layers by holding shift with their keybinds!
When a song is paused, so is the recording!
All keybinds are reconfigurable through this webpage's code.
This project is open source, meaning that you can look at and edit the code as you please!
Bitcrushing is often used to add texture and movement to a song.
When in doubt, bitcrush it out.
Reverb is amazing for creating atmosphere. Almost every threat theme has some!
Reverb is love, reverb is life.
Automation can make songs feel more dynamic and less repetitive!
The majority of threat themes are in a minor key, but the mode (or type) of each varies.
Noise layers are great for filling up empty space and creating a sense of pressure and intensity!
Most threat themes are in 4/4 time, but other time signitures like 5/4 and 4/2 are common as well.
Distortion is what can give basic sounds more grit and power! Like bitcrushing, it can make things sound less "clean" as well.
Rain World has a very unique musical identity, pulling inspiration from numerous genres and allowing for great diversity!
Kicks, snares, and shakers are some examples of percussions, which all serve to create rhythm (what makes you wanna dance!).
Basses, arps, and leads are some examples of layers which serve to create a song's melody (what you hum!).
Weird layers are... well, weird! They can make a song memorable and fun if executed well.
Coral Caves's threat theme is the shortest non-modded/custom song at only 16 seconds in length!
Rusted Canals' and Jeeper's Migration Path threat themes are both the shortest songs on the webpage, being only 7 seconds!
Rusted Basin has the most layers out of any song on the webpage, coming in at a whopping 24!
At 4 minutes and 25 seconds, Intake System takes the throne for the longest threat theme on the webapge!
